markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ repeated delays have pushed Grand Theft Auto VI to a November 19, 2026 release, extending the window for major events to unfold beforehand. Traders are pricing near-certain outcomes such as a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire and Drake’s Iceman album drop, reflecting the extended timeline and real-money consensus on verifiable developments. The game’s path through Take-Two’s earnings updates and Rockstar’s polish-focused statements continues to shape sentiment, with any further shifts in launch timing or confirmed cultural milestones serving as key catalysts ahead of the November target.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' official delays have anchored trader consensus on the "No" outcome for GTA VI launching before June 2026. After shifting from a Fall 2025 window, the studio first moved the open-world sequel to May 26, 2026, then further postponed it to November 19, 2026, citing needs for additional polish. Recent Take-Two earnings calls and Strauss Zelnick statements have reaffirmed this timeline with marketing slated to ramp up this summer, leaving only weeks until June with no credible acceleration signals. While an unprecedented last-minute surprise drop remains theoretically possible in entertainment, historical Rockstar development patterns and locked-in fiscal guidance make any pre-June release highly improbable.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, including the May 2026 hung jury on the remaining rape charge, have driven trader consensus toward No Prison Time at 77.6% implied probability. The disgraced former producer’s 2020 New York conviction was overturned on appeal, while his separate 16-year California sentence faces ongoing challenges amid his age and reported health issues. These factors, combined with mixed 2025 verdicts and stalled prosecutions, position additional lengthy sentences as unlikely in traders’ view. Upcoming developments such as potential plea negotiations or further appeals could still shift outcomes before any final resolution.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2025-26 regular season left the Vegas Golden Knights, Carolina Hurricanes, Colorado Avalanche, and Montreal Canadiens as the four teams with the strongest combination of regular-season point totals, possession metrics, and playoff advancement. Vegas and Carolina sit atop the futures market after strong regular-season finishes and series wins that showcased depth scoring and goaltending stability. Colorado's high regular-season output and star-driven attack keep it competitive despite recent setbacks, while Montreal's young core and upset potential in the Eastern Conference final sustain its share. With conference finals underway, the tight clustering reflects comparable roster health, recent form, and the inherent variance of best-of-seven series at this stage.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France lead the 2026 FIFA World Cup market at nearly identical implied probabilities due to their proven major-tournament pedigree and elite squad depth. Spain enters as Euro 2024 champions with a possession-dominant system, young attacking talent, and an unbeaten run extending into 2026. France follows closely as 2018 winners and 2022 finalists, bolstered by generational depth across positions. England sits third on the back of consecutive European final appearances and a balanced core. The narrow spread among the top three reflects comparable recent form, qualification dominance, and historical knockout resilience, while South American sides trail on less consistent recent results despite individual star power.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s consolidated control over the Chinese Communist Party and Central Military Commission underpins the 93.5% trader consensus against his removal before the end of 2026. Recent military leadership changes, including the January 2026 investigation of longtime ally Zhang Youxia, have been interpreted as further centralization rather than erosion of authority, with no evidence of elite opposition or institutional challenges emerging in credible reporting. High-level diplomatic activity, such as U.S. and Russian summits, signals policy continuity, while the absence of a designated successor and the upcoming 21st Party Congress in late 2027 point to expectations of a fourth term. Periodic health rumors and coup speculation have not altered this positioning, as analysts highlight the lack of verifiable developments capable of triggering removal within the resolution window.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2028 Democratic presidential nomination features a fragmented field in which traders assign Gavin Newsom the leading 24.1% implied probability, reflecting his national profile as California governor and early positioning through media and donor outreach. No other contender exceeds 8.8%, with Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris tied next and a long tail of governors and senators following, underscoring dispersed support ahead of any formal candidacies. With primaries still years away and the 2026 midterms as the next major catalyst, current pricing captures preliminary name recognition and institutional visibility rather than settled voter preferences. Subsequent polling trends, endorsements, and legislative records on key issues could consolidate backing behind a single outcome.
